name: QNI : Access to Improperly Secured Service - Self Signed Certificate
notes: QRadar Network Insights has detected an SSL/TLS session which uses a self-signed certificate. A self-signed certificate in a public-facing or production server application could allow a remote attacker to launch a man-in-the-middle attack.
conditions_text: when the flow matches Flow Direction is any of [R2L or L2R or R2R], Suspect Content Descriptions is not N/A
when any of Suspect Content Descriptions (QNI) match self signed certificate
TD_list: APPLY QNI : Access to Improperly Secured Service - Self Signed Certificate on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the flow matches Flow Direction is any of [R2L or L2R or R2R], Suspect Content Descriptions is not N/A
AND when any of Suspect Content Descriptions (QNI) match self signed certificate
RED_list: Event name: QNI : Access to Improperly Secured Service - Self Signed Certificate
Event description: Detects when an SSL/TLS session uses a self-signed certificate.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
